title: How Iran, Suffering Under Sanctions, Diversified Its Economy
summary: As the nation contended with high inflation, high unemployment and unrest before the war, it became more than just an oil exporter.

title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
summary: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.

title: U.S. Prepares to Blockade Strait of Hormuz, and a MAGA Hero Is Defeated in Europe
summary: Plus, Eric Swalwell suspended his campaign after sexual assault allegations.

title: After Criticizing Pope, Trump Posts Image of Himself as a Jesus-Like Figure
summary: The image, posted on Truth Social, appeared to be A.I.-generated and depicts the president as a divine leader healing the sick.

title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
summary: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.

title: Which Four Countries Pope Leo Is Visiting in Africa, and Why
summary: The pontiff’s journey to the world’s poorest continent will demonstrate his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.

title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that.

title: Defeat of Orban Removes Ukraine’s Biggest Nemesis in Europe
summary: The Hungarian leader’s pro-Moscow tilt cemented his reputation in Kyiv as a spoiler. Now, Ukraine hopes a 90-billion-euro loan he blocked will be disbursed.

title: Hungary May No Longer Be Putin’s Ally, but It Can’t Afford a Full Break
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat will make it harder for the Kremlin to divide Europe, but Moscow retains leverage through energy sales.

title: 4 Takeaways From Orban’s Defeat in Hungary’s Election
summary: Economics and unhappiness with endemic corruption helped Peter Magyar, the challenger, secure victory. A visit by the U.S. vice president in support of Mr. Orban didn’t change things.

title: Eric Swalwell Suspends Campaign for California Governor After Sexual Assault Allegations
summary: In a social media post, the representative said he was “deeply sorry for mistakes in judgment I’ve made in my past,” but promised to fight what he called “false allegations.”

title: California Democrats Rushed to Back Eric Swalwell — and Quickly Abandoned Him
summary: Representative Eric Swalwell, a Washington figure, was largely unknown in the California State Capitol. But influential groups began backing him for governor in the weeks before he was publicly accused of sexual assault.

title: Can Brooklyn’s Grand Army Plaza Be Car-Free? Mamdani Says Yes.
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is expected to propose linking Grand Army Plaza with Prospect Park by closing a dangerous stretch of road between them.

title: Mamdani Returns to the Stage to Tell the Story of His First 100 Days
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered his 100-day address at a concert venue in Queens. He highlighted his accomplishments and was joined by a not-so-surprising guest.

title: Mamdani Plans to Open City-Owned Grocery Store in East Harlem
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced plans for a city-owned grocery store at La Marqueta, moving to deliver on a campaign pledge.

title: How 6 People in New York City Are Coping With Higher Costs
summary: Surging inflation and rising prices have some people re-evaluating date nights and how much of their splurges they can share with friends.

title: ‘The Strangest Sleepover Ever’: Dutch Skeptical as Their Royals Visit Trump
summary: The Dutch king and queen are set to stay at the White House on Monday night. At a time of trans-Atlantic tension, the Netherlands isn’t sure how it feels about that.

title: To Fill Drone Force Ranks, Russia Targets a New Demographic: Students
summary: Recruitment sessions have taken place at hundreds of universities and colleges across the country.

title: Trump’s Military Transgender Ban Is Keeping Workers on Expensive Paid Leave
summary: Highly trained service members have been put on paid leave for nearly a year as they wait for the military to decide their fate.

title: Rejected by Dozens of Emergency Rooms: South Korea’s Medical Crisis
summary: Hundreds of patients every year have trouble finding emergency care, even though the country has a world-class medical system. For some, the delays have been fatal.

title: The Last Day at Kids of Faith: Parents Navigating a Child Care Crisis
summary: Inside one of the hundreds of day care centers that have recently closed in one state, forcing parents to make difficult decisions about their careers.

title: Aiming at China, Malaysia Puts New Restrictions on Electric Cars
summary: A policy move by Malaysia was a tacit acknowledgment of China’s dominance in the global market and the power of its automakers to keep prices low.

title: In Minneapolis, Following Prince’s ‘Purple Trail’
summary: This year is the 10th anniversary of the artist’s death. We made a pilgrimage to the city where he lived and worked.

title: How Trump’s ‘Dealmakers in Peace’ Failed in Iran
summary: Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff thought they could broker peace like a business deal. The war in Iran proves how wrong they were.

title: Your Questions, Answered
summary: You asked about mail-in ballots, oil in the Middle East, manufacturing jobs and more.

title: Elections and Defections Are Likely to Unshackle Canada’s Liberals
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney, who heads a minority government, is expected to gain a majority in the House of Commons after three special elections on Monday.

title: Pearl Fryar, a Picasso of Plants, Dies at 86
summary: A self-taught topiary artist, he discovered a talent for carving trees and shrubs into extraordinary shapes, creating a world-famous garden in a tiny South Carolina town.

title: After Chaotic Election Day, Peru Orders 2nd Day of Voting for Some Areas
summary: Frustrated voters in long lines were unable to cast ballots in parts of the capital, Lima, due to logistical failures, eroding trust for some in the process.

title: The Economist Is Putting Names (and Faces) to Its Magazine
summary: Nameless no more, writers for The Economist are mixing it up on video from its studio in London.
